title: batrachium circinatum var. subrigidum
intro: Batrachium circinatum var. subrigidum, also known as the stiff-leaved frogbit, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Hydrocharitaceae. It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The stiff-leaved frogbit has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions or decoctions, and it can also be dried and used as a tea or added to salves and creams for topical application.
